todaysEvents.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei elected Supreme Leader (Mar 8); IRGC pledged allegiance. Iranian FM Araghchi rejected ceasefire: 'We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people.' No diplomatic off-ramp. (Mar 9)

Iraq oil output down ~60% (Bloomberg, Mar 8); Kuwait cutting as storage fills; JP Morgan projects 4.7M bpd GCC shut-in within 2 weeks. Brent surged to $106.40/bbl. Saudi Arabia struck Mar 8 (Iran-linked, 2 killed). (Mar 9)
todaysEvents.delta: Iraq -60%; Brent $106; GCC 4.7M bpd at risk

todaysEvents.summary: Qatar loaded first LNG cargo since force majeure (partial restart signal, Mar 6). CEO Al-Kaabi: full restart only when conflict ends. IAF struck 400+ targets over Mar 6-7 weekend including Mehrabad Airport and Tehran fuel depots. (Mar 9)
todaysEvents.delta: Qatar first cargo / IAF 400+ targets struck

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Hormuz disruption lasts 2–4 weeks; Qatar FM back-channel eventually leads to ceasefire framework, though Mojtaba Khamenei's election as Supreme Leader (Mar 8) and FM's explicit ceasefire rejection reduce near-term probability. Brent stabilises at $95–110/bbl. Saudi/UAE spare capacity (3-week ramp) partially offsets Iraq/Kuwait supply deficit after Hormuz reopens. ASEAN LNG buyers source Australian and US spot cargoes. P&I clubs restore cover upon ceasefire confirmation. Goldman Q4 2026 normalisation forecast at $60/bbl contingent on swift resolution.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Hormuz closure extends 1–3 months; Indian Ocean front expands with further IRGC naval actions. P&I blackout durable; 329 trapped tankers cleared only partially. Iraq output loss now ~60% (Bloomberg, Mar 8); Kuwait also cutting; GCC shut-in reaching 4.7M bpd per JP Morgan. Brent sustains $100–120/bbl. JKM LNG re-spikes to $22–25/MMBtu as Qatar restart timeline extends. ASEAN current account deficits widen sharply; Bank Indonesia and BSP forced into emergency interventions. Indonesian subsidy blowout accelerates.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Full Hormuz closure sustained beyond 3 months; IRGC acts on Malacca threat; Indian Ocean naval escalation draws in additional parties. Oil at $150–200/bbl (J.P. Morgan upper bound). LNG effectively unavailable for spot buyers; Qatar's 77 mtpa full restart blocked. Iraq, Kuwait, UAE combined output loss exceeds 6M bpd — global supply deficit unprecedented since 1973. Recession probability >70% for Thailand and Philippines. ASEAN EM bonds face mass outflows; P&I cover does not return for 6+ months; global trade finance seizes.
